architecture.summary: Latent-SFT is a three-level framework for training LLMs to perform efficient latent reasoning. At the token level, latent tokens are defined as superpositions within the pre-trained vocabulary embedding space (Latent-Vocab), projected via softmax onto vocabulary logits and reconstructed as linear combinations of token embeddings. At the chain level, explicit reasoning chains are segmented and compressed via Latent Token Induction Mask (LTIM) to create dense representations, then supervised via Latent Token Supervision Mask (LTSuM) to ensure sufficiency for decoding subsequent steps. At the learning level, an EM-style alternating optimization first optimizes an encoder to construct latent chains, then trains the LLM backbone to generate them, followed by joint fine-tuning. During autonomous generation, Latent-Optim uses stochastic Gumbel-Softmax perturbation of target distributions to act as an implicit Hessian regularizer, guiding the model toward flatter minima and enabling generalization across multiple reasoning paths.
trained_components: Latent Token Encoder (shares structure with base LLM)

biblio.title: LLM Latent Reasoning as Chain of Superposition

claims.statement: Latent-SFT(2) consistently outperforms explicit CoT-SFT in Pass@1 scores on nearly all mathematical reasoning datasets, with average Pass@1 improvement of 2.0% while reducing average reasoning chain length by approximately 5.5× on LLaMA-3.2-1B-Instruct
Latent-SFT on DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B surpasses explicit CoT-SFT in Pass@1 performance on high-difficulty tasks while reducing reasoning chain length by 2.7–3.6×
Latent-SFT on DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B achieves Pass@1 of 79.8±1.35 on MATH-500 with 759.3±36.9 token reasoning chain length, outperforming COT-SFT (Pass@1 73.8±1.32, # L 2766±319)
Latent-SFT on DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B achieves Pass@1 of 19.2±1.60 on AIME24 with 1484±420 token reasoning chain length, compared to COT-SFT (Pass@1 17.7±1.23, # L 4016±343)

Removing either Latent Token Induction Mask (LTIM) or Latent Token Supervision Mask (LTSuM) leads to a significant accuracy drop of approximately 5%, confirming their critical role in maintaining structural integrity of the reasoning chain

claims.dataset: GSM8k-Aug, GSM-Hard, SVAMP, MultiArith, MATH-500, AIME24
claims.statement: Replacing Superposition Embedding with raw hidden states degrades performance, demonstrating the importance of aligning latent tokens with the intrinsic vocabulary manifold
Ablating Gumbel-noise injection (w/o Gumbel) leads to a noticeable decline in performance, confirming that stochasticity acts as a vital regularizer to prevent overfitting and enhance generalization

Effective global parallelism (Neff) analysis shows that across models with different compression ratios, most samples exceed the conservative threshold for significant parallelism (1.7), with evidence distributed across roughly 3–4 reasoning paths, confirming latent reasoning reflects superposition of multiple reasoning chains rather than compression of a single path
